nodes.label: Content Authoring Rules
nodes.rationale: Each lesson introduces one concept, reduces support toward mandatory independent code, avoids future constructs, and verifies multiple scenarios when possible.

nodes.label: Practical Python Microlearning
nodes.rationale: Small sequential steps, executable examples, mandatory code, and immediate feedback are intended to build usable skill without overload.

nodes.label: Ranked Spaced Practice Scheduler
nodes.rationale: Practice is ranked by errors, due date, and attempts, uses only completed lessons, alternates formats, and clears a weak item after two consecutive correct solutions.

nodes.label: XP Stars and Streak Progress Model
nodes.norm_label: xp stars and streak progress model

nodes.label: Local Single-User SQLite Progress
nodes.rationale: Progress stays local without registration; tests use a separate temporary database so learner data is not modified.

nodes.label: Local Educational Runner Security Model
nodes.rationale: Layered checks reduce risk for a local trainer but do not provide infrastructure-grade isolation for arbitrary public code.

nodes.label: AST Allowlist Virtual Files and Timeout Controls
nodes.norm_label: ast allowlist virtual files and timeout controls

nodes.label: Local-Only Trust Boundary
nodes.rationale: The current runner must not be exposed as a public multi-tenant arbitrary-code service.

nodes.label: CPU Memory Filesystem Network and Process Limits
nodes.norm_label: cpu memory filesystem network and process limits

nodes.label: Job Queues Rate Limiting and Audit
nodes.norm_label: job queues rate limiting and audit

nodes.label: Private Vulnerability Disclosure
nodes.rationale: Potential vulnerabilities should be reported through a private GitHub security advisory or directly to the repository owner, not in a public issue.

nodes.label: Pull Request Change Rationale and Verification Template
nodes.norm_label: pull request change rationale and verification template

nodes.label: Feature Request Solution and Alternatives Template
nodes.norm_label: feature request solution and alternatives template

nodes.label: Push and Pull Request CI Triggers
nodes.norm_label: push and pull request ci triggers

nodes.label: Lint Format Type and Test Quality Checks
nodes.norm_label: lint format type and test quality checks

nodes.label: Home Practice Projects Sandbox and Reset Navigation
nodes.norm_label: home practice projects sandbox and reset navigation

nodes.label: Intentional Single-User Design
nodes.rationale: Registration and authentication are unnecessary for the current personal trainer, so progress remains in a local database.

nodes.label: Gentle Start Microcourse
nodes.rationale: Twenty early lessons introduce one idea at a time through example, prediction, practice, and repetition before control flow.

nodes.label: Syntax-to-Projects Curriculum Progression
nodes.rationale: The sequence moves from basic syntax through data, tooling, HTTP, SQL, concurrency, async, and graduation projects.

nodes.label: One-Axis Complexity Progression
nodes.rationale: New concepts are separated into steps, support fades gradually, equivalent behavior is accepted, and no construct appears before its introducing lesson.

nodes.label: Six Prerequisite-Gated Project Tools
nodes.rationale: Projects open in sequence only after concrete lesson prerequisites and are checked on multiple scenarios to reject hard-coded demonstration answers.

nodes.label: Delayed Transfer as the Main Learning Metric
nodes.rationale: Success is the ability to solve a new task days later, not opened lessons or correct clicks.
nodes.norm_label: delayed transfer as the main learning metric

nodes.label: One New Concept per Microstep
nodes.rationale: Builtins, methods, operators, syntax forms, execution models, and work techniques each count as separate concepts to control cognitive load.

nodes.label: Concept Prerequisite DAG
nodes.rationale: A directed acyclic graph makes lesson access, practice eligibility, project gates, and reorder safety depend on explicit mastery rather than list position.

nodes.label: No Unfamiliar Constructs Invariant
nodes.rationale: Unknown syntax anywhere in theory, distractors, scaffolds, hints, tests, projects, or solutions creates avoidable cognitive load and is forbidden.

nodes.label: Example-to-Transfer Mastery Ladder
nodes.rationale: Responsibility shifts from a worked example and prediction through modification and guided construction to independent solution and transfer.

nodes.label: PRIMM Learning Cycle
nodes.rationale: Predict, Run, Investigate, Modify, and Make progressively move solution ownership from the course to the learner.

nodes.label: Progressive Hint Fading
nodes.rationale: Hints open one at a time from retrieval cue to near-solution; viewing a full solution requires a later independent task.

nodes.label: Lesson Completion Gate
nodes.rationale: Completion requires at least 75%, correct mandatory independent code, and an actual code run; multiple-choice clicks alone cannot pass.

nodes.label: Spaced Retrieval at 1 3 7 14 and 30 Days
nodes.rationale: A single same-day success is insufficient; concepts return on widening intervals, shortened after errors and extended after confident independent recall.

nodes.label: Adaptive Completed-Lesson Practice
nodes.rationale: Selection uses due date, recency, errors, attempts, and variety; two confident successes remove an item from the error queue.

nodes.label: Behavior-First Evaluation of Equivalent Solutions
nodes.rationale: Multiple inputs, outputs, edge cases, errors, and resource limits determine correctness so equivalent familiar implementations are accepted.

nodes.label: Explicit-Objective AST Structural Check
nodes.rationale: Structural checks are allowed only when the required form is explicitly named as the learning objective, and behavior is still tested.

nodes.label: Seventy-Percent Exam Gate with Mandatory Code
nodes.rationale: Exams test transfer across a module, require all mandatory skills and independent code, and change data on retries.
nodes.norm_label: seventy-percent exam gate with mandatory code

nodes.label: Explicit Project Prerequisites and Multi-Scenario Acceptance
nodes.norm_label: explicit project prerequisites and multi-scenario acceptance

nodes.label: Four-Axis Difficulty Model
nodes.rationale: Syntax novelty, simultaneous concepts, reasoning-chain length, and working-memory/code volume are tracked independently; at most one rises sharply per step.

nodes.label: Learning-First Decision Criteria
nodes.rationale: Prefer fewer simultaneous unknowns, clearer execution models, more retrieval, broader acceptance of correct solutions, concrete feedback, auditability, and preparation for independent work.

nodes.label: Mandatory Human Curriculum Review
nodes.rationale: AST automation cannot detect unclear wording, weak analogies, or excessive cognitive load.

nodes.label: Sweller 1988 Cognitive Load During Problem Solving
nodes.author: John Sweller
nodes.norm_label: sweller 1988 cognitive load during problem solving

nodes.label: Atkinson Renkl and Merrill 2003 Worked Example Fading
nodes.author: Robert K. Atkinson, Alexander Renkl, Mary Margaret Merrill
nodes.norm_label: atkinson renkl and merrill 2003 worked example fading

nodes.label: Renkl et al. 2004 Fading Worked Solution Steps
nodes.author: Alexander Renkl et al.
nodes.norm_label: renkl et al. 2004 fading worked solution steps
